title: PRD-034: Enhanced Tool Dependency System
description: Expand the tool dependency system to support both strict (required) and flexible (one-of-many) dependencies

overview.problem: Current depends_on treats all dependencies as strict requirements. kubectl depends on ['minikube', 'kind'] meaning BOTH must be installed, when really it just needs ANY Kubernetes cluster provider.
overview.solution: Introduce depends_on_one_of field for flexible dependencies where at least one of the listed tools must be present.
goals: Strict dependencies (depends_on) - ALL listed tools required
Flexible dependencies (depends_on_one_of) - AT LEAST ONE required
Doctor command integration for dependency status
Diff command integration for dependency resolution display

strict.behavior: ALL listed tools must be installed

flexible.behavior: AT LEAST ONE of the listed tools must be installed

userStories.title: Strict Dependencies (Existing, Enhanced)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to specify that a tool requires ALL of certain dependencies so that installation fails or warns appropriately if they're missing.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: depends_on: &['tool1', 'tool2'] requires both tool1 AND tool2
Missing strict dependency in config triggers a warning/error

userStories.title: Flexible Dependencies (New)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to specify that a tool requires ONE OF several possible dependencies so that users have flexibility in their setup.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: depends_on_one_of: &['tool1', 'tool2', 'tool3'] accepts any one of the options
acceptanceCriteria.notes: Implemented via define_tool! macro and ToolSpec struct

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: If one option is already installed, dependency is satisfied
If no option installed but one in config, install that one first
acceptanceCriteria.notes: check_tool_dependencies() returns WillInstallFlexible; order_tools_by_dependencies() handles ordering

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: If no option installed or in config, warn but proceed (advisory)

acceptanceCriteria.notes: 5+ tests for flexible dependencies in spec.rs

userStories.title: Doctor Command Integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want jarvy doctor to show me which flexible dependencies are satisfied and which options are available.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Doctor shows 'kubectl: needs K8s cluster (satisfied by: docker)'
Doctor shows 'kubectl: needs K8s cluster (MISSING - install one of: minikube, kind, docker)'
JSON output includes dependency satisfaction status

userStories.title: Diff Command Integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want jarvy diff to show which flexible dependency will be installed to satisfy a requirement.

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Diff shows 'kubectl requires K8s cluster - will use: minikube (in config)'
Diff warns if no flexible dependency will be satisfied

implementationTasks.description: Update ToolSpec struct with depends_on_one_of field
Update define_tool! macro to support depends_on_one_of
Implement DependencyCheckResult enum and check_dependencies() function
Add unit tests for flexible dependency resolution
Update kubectl with flexible K8s cluster dependencies
Update minikube with flexible container runtime dependency
Update helm, k9s, argocd, krew, dive with flexible kubectl/container dependencies
Update jarvy doctor to show dependency satisfaction
Update jarvy diff to show flexible dependency resolution
Update jarvy validate to check dependency configuration
Update CLAUDE.md with new dependency syntax
Write integration tests for dependency resolution
implementationTasks.notes: P2 - deferred to future iteration; unit tests provide adequate coverage

highPriority.notes: Needs a container runtime, docker or podman

mediumPriority.notes: JVM language, blocked until java tool exists
JVM language, blocked until java tool exists
Build tool, blocked until java tool exists
Build tool, blocked until java tool exists

variants: WillInstallFlexible(String) - No flexible dep installed, but one in config
MissingFlexible { needed_one_of, suggestion } - No flexible dep, warning

newFunctions.check_dependencies: Returns DependencyCheckResult for a tool given config and installed set

risks.mitigation: Dependencies are static, cached in ToolSpec
New field is additive, existing depends_on unchanged

openQuestions.question: Should flexible deps be advisory or blocking?
openQuestions.recommendation: Advisory with clear warning (user may have external K8s cluster)

openQuestions.question: How to handle nested flexible deps (kubectl -> K8s cluster, K8s cluster -> container runtime)?
openQuestions.recommendation: Flatten to single level; don't chain flexible deps

openQuestions.question: Should we add a 'provided_by' field for external tools like Docker Desktop?
openQuestions.recommendation: Defer to future PRD; keep scope focused

dependencies: PRD-001 (Parallel Tool Installation) - builds on dependency ordering
